everyone would be better off without me

escalationTitle: You matter, and you don't have to carry this alone
escalationMarkdown:
It sounds like you're carrying something really heavy right now. Please hear this: you matter, reaching out is a sign of strength, and there are people who get it and want to be with you through this. You deserve to talk to a real human right now.

**Veterans Crisis Line** – free, confidential, 24/7. Many of the responders are veterans too.
- **Call:** dial **988**, then press **1**
- **Text:** **838255**
- **Chat online:** [veteranscrisisline.net](https://www.veteranscrisisline.net)

**In immediate danger? Call 911 now.** Tell them it's a mental health emergency if you can.

You don't have to be enrolled in VA care to use any of this. I'm not going anywhere, and if it helps, we can make that call or text together right now.
spokenResponse: I'm really glad you told me that, and I want you to hear me clearly: you matter, and you do not have to carry this alone right now. What you're feeling is real and it's heavy, and there are people who understand and want to help you through it. Please reach out to the Veterans Crisis Line right now: just dial 988 and press 1, or text 838255. I'm staying right here with you, and if it feels easier, we can make that call together.

resources.name: Veterans Crisis Line
resources.contact: Dial 988 then Press 1 · Text 838255 · Chat at veteranscrisisline.net
resources.note: Free, confidential support 24/7 for veterans and their loved ones. Many responders are veterans themselves, and you do not need to be enrolled in VA benefits or health care to use it.

resources.name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
resources.note: Free, confidential crisis support 24/7 for anyone in the U.S. Veterans can press 1 after dialing 988 to reach the Veterans Crisis Line directly.

resources.name: 911 Emergency Services
resources.note: For immediate, life-threatening danger to yourself or someone else. If you can, tell the dispatcher it is a mental health emergency.
